currentGoal: Keep the dashboard and docs focused on Luna's plain-language welcome, one clear Next Action, the stable Philosophy Garden cycle, calm guidance, and public-safe visual identity direction.
lastCompletedRun: 2026-07-09: Reviewed and lightly clarified the visual identity note so it matches Luna's small public dashboard direction and keeps visual personality focused on returning, finding one safe next step, and avoiding overwhelm.
nextStep: Rest or review documentation clarity before considering any visual identity styling.
dashboardGuide: Welcome explains Luna in plain language before the project details begin.
Status gives a quick snapshot of how the project is doing right now.
Current Goal is the main thing Luna is focused on.
Last Completed Run shows the most recent finished update.
Next Action highlights the single safest action for a beginner to take next.
Read the dashboard from top to bottom; if you only need one task, stop at Next Action.
futureIdeas: Rest or review documentation clarity before considering any visual identity styling.
Review one existing visual or interface sentence later for beginner-friendly clarity.
Keep project.json, STATE.md, and CHANGELOG.md aligned after each small dashboard or documentation change.
decisionLog: A dashboard makes Luna easy to understand at a glance.
Structured project data keeps updates small, safe, and beginner-friendly.
Showing goals, ideas, and decisions first creates a clear trail before adding larger features.
The Next Action card helps beginners choose one clear next move without sorting through every project note.
The Philosophy Garden seed gives Luna one tiny creative direction while staying public-safe and beginner-friendly.
The first Reflection Card gives visitors one gentle question to consider without asking for private personal details.
The Garden Path keeps Philosophy Garden updates slow, public-safe, and easy for beginners to follow.
The Garden Review helps Luna learn from the first Reflection Card before choosing the next gentle public-safe update.
The Garden Cycle connects the Reflection Card, Garden Review, Next Garden Update, and rest step so beginners can see how Philosophy Garden pieces fit together.

ideaQueueGuide: Choose one small task that can be finished clearly in a single update.

Keep private details out of the public repo.

glossary.meaning: A project folder that keeps files and their change history together.
A safe workspace for trying changes without changing the main version right away.
A request to review and bring a set of changes into the project.
To combine approved changes from one branch into another branch.
The one safest small task Luna suggests doing next, so beginners do not have to choose from every idea at once.

localSetup: Open a terminal in the Luna project folder.
Start a simple local web server with python3 -m http.server 8000.
Visit http://localhost:8000 in a browser to view the dashboard.

creativeSeed.name: Philosophy Garden
creativeSeed.description: A tiny public-safe space for simple reflection prompts, gentle questions, and beginner-friendly ideas.
ideas: Write one calm question about how a small project grows.
Add a tiny reflection card about choosing the next kind action.
Collect one beginner-friendly thought experiment with no personal details.

reflectionCard.title: Notice the Small Gate
reflectionCard.question: What is one small, kind step that could help this project grow without rushing?
reflectionCard.whyItMatters: This question matters because gentle growth helps Luna stay clear, welcoming, and easy for beginners to follow.
reflectionCard.beginnerNote: Answer with a general project idea or kind next step; do not include private personal details.

gardenReview.title: Review the Small Gate
gardenReview.lesson: The first Reflection Card teaches that Luna can grow through one small, kind step instead of rushing into bigger changes.
gardenReview.nextUpdate: Next, choose one gentle public-safe update that keeps the Philosophy Garden easy for beginners to follow.

nextGardenUpdate.title: Name One Gentle Growth Step
nextGardenUpdate.purpose: Help visitors turn the Garden Review lesson into one small, kind project action without rushing the Philosophy Garden.
nextGardenUpdate.suggestedAction: Choose one general, public-safe improvement that makes the dashboard easier for beginners to follow.

gardenCycle.title: Reflection Card
gardenCycle.sentence: Start with one gentle public-safe question for the project.

gardenCycle.title: Garden Review
gardenCycle.sentence: Review what the reflection card teaches in simple beginner-friendly words.

gardenCycle.title: Next Garden Update
gardenCycle.sentence: Choose one small public-safe action that grows from the review.

gardenCycle.title: Rest Before Growing Again
gardenCycle.sentence: Let the garden rest before adding another prompt or reflection card.

gardenPath: Plant one small public-safe reflection card.
Let the card rest before changing the garden again.
Review what the card teaches in simple beginner-friendly words.
Choose one gentle update for the next small growth step.

welcome.title: Welcome to Luna
welcome.sentence: Luna is a small project dashboard that shows what changed, what matters now, and one safe next step.
